title: Breakout-Retest with Volatility Expansion and Mechanical Bad-Regime Gate -- frozen strategy spec (pre-registration)

design_context.must_avoid_donchian: Donchian's RAW breakout entry was ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED and SEQUENCE_RISK_FRAGILE (net flips negative without the top 3 winners). S28 does NOT enter the breakout; it enters the retest-that-holds and pre-registers a significance test that the retest must BEAT the raw breakout.
design_context.must_avoid_s26: S26 was FRICTION_SENSITIVE, REGIME_RISK_INCONCLUSIVE, and collapsed in the 2014-2016 chop with no no-trade gate. S28 adds an explicit mechanical bad-regime NO-TRADE gate and uses wider-structure stops for friction headroom.
design_context.must_avoid_s27: S27 mean-reversion FAILED IS with only 18 trades and negative expectancy (capped target rarely reached, two-step confirmation too restrictive). S28 pre-registers explicit trade-count floors and keeps the entry selective-but-not-rare; +2R target on larger structures.
design_context.entry_must_be_significance_testable: The retest-hold is a discrete datable event tested two ways: vs random-in-universe AND vs raw-breakout entry, at fixed horizons.

spec.1_strategy_name: Breakout-Retest with Volatility Expansion and Mechanical Bad-Regime Gate

2_market_and_timeframe.independent_confirmation_later: ES daily (D10 only; never part of the entry)
2_market_and_timeframe.OOS: 2023-2025 (SEALED until the OOS protocol step is committed)

spec.3_core_hypothesis: A raw breakout alone is noisy, but a breakout that expands volatility/participation, then RETESTS and HOLDS the prior resistance level, may identify genuine acceptance above resistance (prior resistance becomes support). The edge must come from the retest-hold event AFTER expansion -- not from raw breakout chasing -- which is exactly why entry significance is pre-registered against BOTH random-in-universe and raw-breakout entries.

4_trend_background_filter.why: Long-only context. The slope gate (consistent with the lesson from S26/S27) restricts to a genuinely rising trend, not a topping/rolling market, and is a fixed mechanical binary gate -- no optimization.

5_resistance_and_breakout.resistance_definition: highest HIGH over the prior 55 bars, EXCLUDING the current bar
5_resistance_and_breakout.breakout_rule: a bar is a BREAKOUT bar if close > resistance
5_resistance_and_breakout.range_expansion_required: true range of the breakout bar >= 1.25 * ATR20 (ATR20 through the breakout bar)
5_resistance_and_breakout.volume_expansion_required: volume of the breakout bar >= 1.20 * SMA20(volume) (20-bar simple average of volume, prior+current)
5_resistance_and_breakout.volume_source: real daily volume column (present in the provisioned daily CSVs)
5_resistance_and_breakout.volume_fallback: If a future data concern makes volume unusable, a range-expansion-only variant (TR >= 1.25*ATR20) would be a SEPARATE branch, not a silent fallback. v1 REQUIRES volume expansion.

6_retest_window.window_bars: up to 10 bars AFTER the breakout bar
6_retest_window.retest_rule: on a candidate bar j in the window: low[j] <= resistance + 0.25 * ATR20_at_breakout
6_retest_window.hold_rule: AND close[j] >= resistance  (the retest bar closes back above the reclaimed level)
6_retest_window.signal_bar: the first bar in the window satisfying BOTH retest and hold = the retest-hold (signal) bar
6_retest_window.expiry_no_retest: if no bar in the 10-bar window satisfies the retest rule, the setup EXPIRES
6_retest_window.expiry_failure: if close < resistance on any in-window bar BEFORE a retest-hold occurs, the setup FAILS/expires (acceptance lost)
6_retest_window.one_active_setup: only one pending breakout setup is tracked at a time

7_entry_timing.entry: next bar open = open of (signal bar + 1)

8_bad_regime_no_trade_gate.rule: NO TRADE (setup is suppressed) if  abs(close - EMA200) <= 1.0 * ATR20  AND  (EMA50[i] - EMA50[i-20]) <= 0
8_bad_regime_no_trade_gate.why_A: Directly targets the documented 2014-2016 failure regime (price oscillating near the 200MA with a flat/falling intermediate trend). Genuine trend breakouts of 55-day highs sit well above EMA200, so this gate rarely blocks real trends and mainly blocks the near-mean chop that sank S26/Donchian. Mechanical, stdlib (EMA arrays already computed), no subjective labels.

9_stop.rule: stop = entry - 2.0 * ATR20 (ATR20 as of the retest-hold signal bar)
9_stop.R_unit: 1R = 2.0 * ATR20 = the stop distance. All results in R only.
9_stop.why_atr_based: ATR-based stop gives clean, consistent R accounting and wider headroom on larger breakout structures (friction headroom that S26 lacked).

10_exit.time_stop: exit at the CLOSE of the 20th bar held if neither stop nor target hit
10_exit.failure_exit_close_below_resistance: NOT used in v1 (deliberately omitted to keep the exit fixed and simple, mirroring S26/S27 v1 minimalism). Adding it later opens a new branch.
10_exit.exit_precedence: stop -> target -> time_stop. If stop and target are both touched on the same bar, STOP fills first (conservative).
10_exit.management_begins: the bar AFTER the fill bar (conservative; the fill bar opens the position but is not itself an exit bar)

11_position_rules: one active setup at a time

12_entry_significance_protocol.test_1: retest-hold entries vs the SAME COUNT of random entries drawn from the same trend/regime-allowed universe (bull filter true, bad-regime gate passed)
12_entry_significance_protocol.test_2: retest-hold entries vs RAW BREAKOUT entries (enter at the breakout bar's next open) -- isolates the retest's marginal edge AND tests against the parked Donchian style
12_entry_significance_protocol.requirement_note: The retest must beat random AND beat the raw breakout to be EDGE_LIKELY. If it does not beat raw-breakout, PARK -- do not continue 'inconclusive' like S26.

13_validation_ladder.D7: entry significance (retest vs random AND vs raw breakout)

14_pass_watch_fail_thresholds.note: Pre-registered BEFORE any results. Conservative. PASS = all met; WATCH = minor misses; FAIL = any hard floor breached.

max_dd_cap.pass: IS max DD <= IS net R
max_dd_cap.fail: IS max DD > 1.5x IS net R

top3_winner_dependence_cap.pass: IS net stays POSITIVE after removing top 3 winners
top3_winner_dependence_cap.fail: IS net goes negative without top 3 (Donchian failure mode)

entry_significance_requirement.pass: EDGE_LIKELY vs random AND beats raw-breakout
entry_significance_requirement.fail: NOT_SUPPORTED, or does not beat raw breakout

friction_requirement.pass: IS positive at 0.05R AND OOS positive at 0.10R per trade

15_anti_overfit_rules: no changing the breakout length (55), ATR multipliers (1.25 TR, 0.25 retest, 2.0 stop), volume multiplier (1.20), retest window (10), time stop (20), or regime-gate constants after seeing results
no OOS peeking before the OOS protocol is committed
no adding or removing filters after OOS
if any rule changes, open a NEW branch (do not mutate S28 in place)
no paper/live promotion from this spec or from IS results
16_expected_failure_modes: too few trades (selective stack: 55-bar high + range + volume + retest + regime gate) -- watch the IS/OOS trade floors
retest rule too strict (price breaks out and never returns within 10 bars) -- expiry rate may be high
volume filter too restrictive (kills otherwise-valid retests) -- watch trade count
late entries after the move is exhausted (retest happens far into the move) -- watch win rate vs time-stop exits
friction sensitivity -- mitigated by the wider 2.0*ATR stop but stress-tested at D12
false breakouts in chop -- mitigated by the bad-regime no-trade gate + retest-hold requirement
dependence on post-2016 trend regimes -- D9 regime breakdown checks 2014-2016 explicitly
spec.17_final_line: S28-D2 is a frozen hypothesis specification only; no strategy code, backtest, OOS, paper, or live trading is authorized.

trading_recommendation: NONE. Frozen spec only. Donchian, S26, and S27 stay PARKED; S28 is an unbuilt, unvalidated hypothesis.
